channel.title: SOC Intelligence Digest
channel.description: Seven-day operational threat intelligence and IOC reporting for SOC review. Coverage window: 2026/07/15 18:39 - 2026/07/22 18:39 JST. Article dates: 2026/07/15 - 2026/07/22 JST.

item.title: Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Vulnerability, Exploit, Patch, Ransomware, Threat Actor / Topics: Network Edge, Ransomware, Vulnerability, Exploit / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (82) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 2 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, compound vulnerability exploit impact detection context, reported by 2 sources, primary references 1 / Summary: Threat actors have been observed exploiting a now-patched high-severity Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS vulnerability as an entry point to deploy Qilin (aka Agenda) ransomware on victim environments. Arctic Wolf Labs said it investigated multiple intrusions in June 2026 that began with the exploitation of CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), an authentication bypass flaw affecting the portal and gateway / Also reported by: Security Affairs / Primary source: ArcticWolf https://arcticwolf.com/resources/blog/exploitation-of-cve-2026-0257-leads-to-qilin-ransomware/

item.title: Your AI agent’s config is now the payload: How attackers are targeting the developer agent harness
item.description: Source: Tenable / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Exposure Management, Threat Intel, Malware, Supply Chain, Identity, AI Security / Topics: Microsoft, Supply Chain, Malware, Identity, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: Attackers have shifted from hiding from AI tools to running inside them. By poisoning the config files that govern AI coding assistants, a new worm class achieves silent persistence, evades AI-based scanners, and spreads across an organization's repositories through developers' own tools. Key takeaways AI coding assistant configuration files, such as settings.json hooks, .cursorrules Cursor MDC rules, and similar harness files, are now explicit targets in supply-chain attacks, not collateral damage. These files simultaneously sit at the intersection of three trust relationships: The developer / Primary source: Tenable https://www.tenable.com/blog/ai-coding-assistant-agent-harness-attacks / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Estée Lauder Confirms Cyberattack Affecting Personal Information
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Security, Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Ransomware, Identity, Data Breach, Network / Topics: Network Edge, Ransomware, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Identity, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (77) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: The Estée Lauder data breach has prompted the global cosmetics company to notify affected individuals after hackers exploited a vulnerability in Oracle E-Business Suite, a platform used for human resources (HR) operations. The Estée Lauder cyberattack stemmed from unauthorized access that occurred on or around August 9, 2025, though the company said it identified the incident last month and confirmed the scope of the breach on June 19, 2026. Estée Lauder Data Breach Exposed Sensitive Personal Information According to the company's notification letter , the attackers gained access to the Oracle / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/cybercrime/fraud/

item.title: Click to Sync: From Google Ads Maintenance Notice to Credential Theft
item.description: Source: Cofense / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Phishing, Identity / Topics: Phishing, Identity / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN critical sector, campaign priority, IOC 3, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: The Cofense Phishing Defense Center (PDC) has observed a new phishing campaign targeting Google Ads Sync Accounts (MMC), in which attackers send fake system upgrade notifications that urge recipients to synchronize their accounts immediately. Brand impersonation remains one of the most common tactics used to establish trust with victims. / Primary source: Cofense https://cofense.com/blog/click-to-sync-from-google-ads-maintenance-notice-to-credential-theft / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Targeted Attack on Government Entities in the Middle East | Part 1
item.description: Source: Zscaler / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, APT, Threat Actor, Endpoint, Network, Application Security / Topics: Microsoft, Malware, APT / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: APT / targeted campaign (rank 6) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: APT / targeted campaign, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN targeted/APT/state-backed, EN Japan/Asia relevance, EN critical sector, JA attack activity, APT/campaign priority, IOC 12, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, Japan related, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: IntroductionIn July 2026, Zscaler ThreatLabz observed new activity by a threat actor with links to East Asia targeting government entities in the Middle East. During analysis, ThreatLabz captured post-compromise activity and uncovered previously undocumented malware tooling, including TELESHIM, MIXEDKEY, and BINDCLOAK. The campaign used a multi-stage attack chain to establish and maintain access on infected systems, with TELESHIM abusing the Telegram API for command-and-control (C2) communication to blend in with legitimate internet traffic.This blog post (Part 1) explores the technical detail / Primary source: Zscaler https://www.zscaler.com/blogs/security-research/targeted-attack-government-entities-middle-east-part-1 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: APTグループ「Sednit」が活動を再開ークラウドを活用した高度な攻撃手法が判明
item.description: Source: Canon MJ ESET Security / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Exploit, Phishing, APT, Threat Actor, Endpoint, Incident Response, Application Security / Topics: APT28, Linux, Malware, Exploit, Phishing, APT / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: APT / targeted campaign (rank 6) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: APT / targeted campaign, EN targeted/APT/state-backed, EN Japan/Asia relevance, JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, JA targeted/APT/state-backed, JA critical sector, APT/campaign priority, IOC 3, linked IOC evidence, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, Japan related, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: ESET社は、2つのインプラント「BeardShell」と「Covenant」を中心とした「デュアル（二重）インプラント」手法を利用するSednitの高度なインプラントチームが活動を再開したことを発見しました。 本記事では、その手法の特徴と攻撃の実態について解説します。 本記事はESET Japanが提供する「ESETブログ」に掲載された「Sednit再始動：戦線に復帰」を再編集したものです。 2024年4月以降、Sednitの高度な技術力を持つ開発チームが再始動しました。このチームは、レジリエンスを高める目的で異なるクラウドプロバイダーを利用する2つのインプラント「BeardShell」と「Covenant」を中心とした、最新のツールキットを携えています。この「デュアル（二重）インプラント」手法により、ウクライナ軍関係者に対する長期的な監視が可能となりました。興味深いことに、これら最新のツールセットのコードには、2010年代に同グループが使用していたインプラントの直接的な系譜が見て取れます。 本ブログの要点： ESET社の研究チームは、キーロガー「SlimAgent」がウクライナで展開された2024年の事例をきっかけに、Sednitの高度なインプラントチームが活動を再開したことを突き止めました SlimAgentのコードは、2010年代のSednitの主要なバックドアであったXa / Primary source: Canon MJ ESET Security https://eset-info.canon-its.jp/malware_info/special/detail/260721.html / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: HollowGraph Malware Hides C2 and Stolen Files in Microsoft 365 Events Dated 2050

Group-IB, which named the malware HollowGraph, says the approach moves tasking and stolen data through legitimate Microsoft Graph API traffic, so the activity looks / Primary source: Group-IB https://www.group-ib.com/blog/hollowgraph-microsoft-365/

item.title: From a Single Alert to 1,000 Files: Inside an Exposed WebDAV Malware Delivery Lab
item.description: Source: Rapid7 / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Intel, Research, Vulnerability, Data Breach, AI Security, Endpoint, Network, Detection / Topics: Microsoft, Phishing, Malware, Vulnerability, Data Breach, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, IOC 13, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: Executive summary An MDR alert recently led our team to an exposed server that was doing more than hosting payloads. It was functioning as a fully operational malware delivery lab. Containing over 1,000 artifacts, the infrastructure served as a QA hub where attackers systematically tested delivery paths, social engineering lures, and WebDAV execution methods. Our analysis reveals an interesting shift in adversary operations: attackers are adopting generative AI to move beyond individual exploits and operate like modern software product teams. By leveraging LLMs for rapid lure generation, detai / Primary source: Rapid7 https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/tr-exposed-webdav-malware-delivery-lab-analysis / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Fairlife Ransomware Attack Hits Production Systems, U.S. Operations Suspended
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Ransomware, Network, Incident Response / Topics: Network Edge, Ransomware / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (86) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN Japan/Asia relevance, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, Japan related, primary references 3 / Summary: The Fairlife ransomware attack has temporarily halted production operations at Coca-Cola -owned dairy company fairlife in the United States after unauthorized access was detected in a portion of its systems, including production-related systems. According to The Coca-Cola Company, fairlife identified unauthorized access by a third party in connection with a ransomware event. Following the discovery, the company activated its incident response and business continuity protocols while launching an investigation with the support of external advisors and cybersecurity experts. Law enforcement has a / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/knowledge-hub/what-is-ransomware/

According to the Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA), the activity has been attributed to UAC-0145, a sub-cluster within Sandworm, an advanced hacking unit affiliated with GRU, Russia's / Primary source: cert.gov.ua https://cert.gov.ua/article/6318437

item.title: WordPress Coreの脆弱性 CVE-2026-63030 / CVE-2026-60137 (通称「wp2shell」)についてまとめてみた
item.description: Source: piyolog / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: AI Security, Vulnerability, Exploit, Patch, Threat Actor, Threat Intel, Incident Response, Application Security / Topics: CVE-2026-60137, CVE-2026-63030, Web Application, AI Security, Vulnerability, Exploit / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 2 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, JA attack activity, JA IOC/detection/hunting, JA active exploit/PoC/zero-day, JA detection/analysis, JA Japan/Asia relevance, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, compound vulnerability exploit impact detection context, indicator context, Japan related, reported by 2 sources, primary references 1 / Summary: 2026年7月17日、WordPressを開発するWordPress.orgは、WordPress Coreに認証不要のリモートコード実行(RCE)に至る深刻な脆弱性が確認されたとして、セキュリティリリース版WordPress 7.0.2を公開し、対象バージョンを利用するサイトに自動更新システム経由での強制アップデートを有効化する対応を取りました。これら2件の脆弱性を組み合わせた攻撃は、発見者により「wp2shell」と命名されています。ここでは関連する情報をまとめます。 どんな脆弱性が確認されたの? WordPress Coreでは、REST APIのバッチ処理において、サブリクエストと対応するルート・ハンドラー・検証結果の対応関係がずれる不具合(CVE-2026-63030)と、WP_Queryのauthor__not_inパラメータに攻撃者が制御可能なスカラー値が渡された場合にSQLインジェクションが生じる不具合(CVE-2026-60137)が確認された。 CVE-2026-63030を悪用すると、本来Batch API経由では呼び出せないREST APIエンドポイントの実行や、パラメータの存在確認、型チェック、権限確認など、エンドポイント実行前の検証を迂回できる。CVE-2026-60137と組み合わせることで、影響を受けるWordPress 6.9.0〜6.9.4および / Also reported by: Rapid7 / Primary source: piyolog https://piyolog.hatenadiary.jp/entry/2026/07/19/181822 / Primary source age: 0 days

The malicious package campaign, codenamed ViteVenom by Checkmarx, marks an expansion of ChainVeil, which was observed using an "unprecedented" four-tier blockchain-based command-and-control (C2) infrastructure spanning Tron, / Primary source: opensourcemalware.com https://opensourcemalware.com/blog/chainveil-and-vitevenom-dprk-polinrider-campaign

item.title: CVE-2026-58644: Microsoft SharePoint Server Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
item.description: Source: Rapid7 / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, Vulnerability, Exploit, Patch, Network, Incident Response, Application Security / Topics: CVE-2026-58644, Microsoft, Web Application, Vulnerability, Exploit / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: active exploitation (rank 4) / Reference count: 2 / Reasons: focus: active exploitation, EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, active exploitation priority, target and impact context, detection evidence context, vulnerability exploitation context, compound vulnerability exploit impact detection context, indicator context, reported by 2 sources, primary references 1 / Summary: Overview On July 14, 2026, Microsoft published a security advisory addressing CVE-2026-58644 , a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability affecting on-premises Microsoft SharePoint Server deployments. The vulnerability, which carries a CVSS v3.1 score of 9.8 (Critical), results from the deserialization of untrusted data ( CWE-502 ) and allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code. Microsoft confirmed active exploitation of CVE-2026-58644, and the vulnerability was subsequently added to CISA’s Known Exploited Vulnerabilities ( KEV ) catalog on July 16, 2026. In paralle / Also reported by: The HackersNews / Primary source: Rapid7 https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/etr-cve-2026-58644-microsoft-sharepoint-server-unauthenticated-remote-code-execution-vulnerability-exploited-in-the-wild / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: ACR Stealer: Two observed intrusion chains amid increased threat activity

item.description: Source: Zscaler / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Threat Actor, Data Breach, AI Security, Detection, Cryptocurrency, Application Security / Topics: Apple/iOS, Malware, Data Breach, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, IOC 209, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, primary references 1 / Summary: IntroductionClickFix is a widely employed attack technique, first seen in 2024, where a victim is instructed to paste-and-run instructions on their system to “fix” a problem or install software. The seemingly benign instructions are, in fact, malicious and lead to the deployment of malware onto the victim’s system. Zscaler Threat Hunting has identified recent ClickFix attacks abusing Anthropic’s Claude platform through the use of shareable Claude chats to host these instructions, which marks a shift from typical attacks. item.title: 「閉域環境」でも導入できる オンプレミス型EDRで守る工場セキュリティ
item.description: Source: Cybereason / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Threat Intel, ICS/OT, Endpoint, Incident Response / Topics: Operational Technology, Ransomware, ICS/OT / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 95 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, JA critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, detection evidence context, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: セキュリティインシデントの傾向 ランサムウェアの被害は2025年上半期に116件と過去最多水準に達しており、いつ自組織が被害を受けてもおかしくない状況です。特に製造業は、他の業種と異なり工場（OT）への攻撃自体が脅迫の材料になるため攻撃者からも注視されています。被害件数を見ても製造業が45％程度を占めています。 生産現場が狙われる構造の変化 生産現場における構造は2種の変化があると考えています。 １つ目は、今まで万全と思われていた壁がデジタル化によって「透明な壁」に変わっています。 2つ目は、変化と表現しておりますが、工場特有の制約が招く「セキュリティの空白地帯」がございます。 この「セキュリティの空白地帯」は次の様な理由から攻撃者の標的となり得ます。 工場セキュリティガイドラインが求める「３つの柱」 我々は、今後の工場を守るための工場セキュリティガイドラインから「３つの柱」が必要であると考えています。 本Blogでは、この「3つの柱」を実現するための２種のサービスを紹介します。 オンプレミス型EDR オンプレミス型EDRをご活用頂くことで、閉域環境でもクラウド環境と同等のセキュリティを維持することができます。これにより、クラウド型EDRの導入が難しい場合においても、攻撃者の動向を監視することが可能となります。 また、オンプレミス型EDRにて可視化する範囲は以下を想定しております。 / Primary source: Cybereason https://www.cybereason.co.jp/blog/product/edr/14348/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Investigating Persistence Mechanisms in AWS
item.description: Source: Rapid7 / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, Threat Actor, Cloud, Identity, Detection, Application Security / Topics: Cloud Security, Cloud, Identity / Priority: Critical (88) / SOC: 74 / Confidence: High (96) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, target and impact context, uncertain reporting, primary references 1 / Summary: Overview In the cloud, your infrastructure may be short-lived, but an attacker’s persistence doesn't have to be. While your environment scales and changes in seconds, adversaries are embedding themselves into your IAM policies, Lambda functions, and federated sessions, creating invisible footholds that survive long after you believe an incident is closed. Persistence in AWS is not just a technical oversight; it is a fundamental business risk. If you cannot see how an attacker has rooted themselves in your environment, you cannot contain them. This article moves beyond theory to provide the cri / Primary source: Rapid7 https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/dr-investigating-aws-persistence-mechanisms / Primary source age: 0 days

item.description: Source: ANY.RUN / Source attributes: United Arab Emirates / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Malware, Security, Threat Intel, Phishing, Identity / Topics: Microsoft, Malware, Phishing, Identity / Priority: Critical (87) / SOC: 71 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, campaign priority, IOC 35, coherent attack event, target and impact context, primary references 1 / Summary: Kali365 is targeting US organizations with device code phishing attacks that abuse legitimate Microsoft authentication. Instead of directing victims to a fake login form, the phishkit sends them to a real Microsoft page, where they authorize access using an attacker-controlled device code. This makes the attack harder for analysts to spot and more dangerous for the business. By obtaining OAuth access and refresh tokens, attackers may gain continued access to Microsoft 365 […] The post Kali365 Targets US Organizations with Data Theft via Device Code Phishing appeared first on ANY.RUN's Cybersec / Primary source: ANY.RUN https://any.run/cybersecurity-blog/kali365-phishing-targeting-us/ / Primary source age: 0 days

To keep pace, many security teams are exploring how to integrate large language model (LLM) agents into their codebases, development environments and continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines for automated vulnerability discovery and remediation. item.title: CVE-2026-42533 Exposes Critical Pre-Auth nginx RCE Flaw
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intel, Exploit, Patch, Data Breach, Network / Topics: CVE-2026-42533, Network Edge, Vulnerability, Exploit, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (83) / SOC: 61 / Confidence: Medium (67) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, uncertain reporting, primary references 1 / Summary: A newly disclosed security flaw, CVE-2026-42533, has revealed a critical Pre-Auth nginx vulnerability that could allow attackers to achieve reliable RCE (remote code execution) without authentication. The issue affects nginx versions 0.9.6 through 1.30.3 (stable) and 1.31.2 (mainline), while patched releases include 1.30.4 and 1.31.3. Affected NGINX Plus versions include R33-R36 (fixed in R36 P7) and 37.0.0.1-37.0.2.1 (fixed in 37.0.3.1). According to the disclosure, the vulnerability stems from a missing save-and-restore mechanism for PCRE capture state within nginx's two-pass script evaluati / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/resources/research-reports/

item.title: RondoDox to begin DDoS attacks
item.description: Source: NICTER Blog / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Detection, Exploit, Malware, DDoS, Cryptocurrency / Topics: Exploit, Malware / Priority: Critical (82) / SOC: 92 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: IOC / detection evidence (rank 2) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: IOC / detection evidence, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, JA attack activity, IOC 15, linked IOC evidence, indicator context, Japan source, primary references 1, IOC/detection-only priority cap / Summary: On January 13 2026, we have only reported that the RondoDox is mining Monero 1 though it has the ability of DDoS attack like the other IoT botnets. The reason we didn’t report about the DDoS attack functionality was that the malware itself was only keeping a connection with the command and control (C2) server by repeating keepalive commands except a few DDoS attack trials while the downloaded mining tool was running separately for a long time. About 10 months after the massive exploitation in July 2025, NICT Cyber Threat analysis Office has identidied that the RondoDox C2 began to send the DDo / Primary source: NICTER Blog https://blog.nicter.jp/2026/07/rondodox_2026_3g/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: RondoDoxがDDoS攻撃を開始
item.description: Source: NICTER Blog / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Detection, Malware, DDoS, Cryptocurrency / Topics: Malware / Priority: Critical (81) / SOC: 93 / Confidence: High (93) / Focus: IOC / detection evidence (rank 2) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: IOC / detection evidence, EN attack activity, JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, IOC 14, detection evidence context, indicator context, Japan source, primary references 1, IOC/detection-only priority cap / Summary: 2026年1月13日に RondoDox について報告しましたが 1 、実質的に暗号通貨 Monero のマイニングに特化していたため、当時は DDoS 機能については触れませんでした。他の IoT マルウェア同様に本体には DDoS 機能が実装されていますが、マイニングツールをダウンロードして起動した後は、DDoS 攻撃はほとんど観測されず、C2との接続を維持するために keepalive コマンドを定期的に繰り返すだけでした。しかし、5月になり、NICT サイバー脅威分析室は、RondoDox が DDoS 攻撃を開始したことを確認しました。2025年7月の大規模なエクスプロイトから約10ヶ月も経っていました。 / Primary source: NICTER Blog https://blog.nicter.jp/2026/07/rondodox_2026_3gj/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: サイバー攻撃によるニチレイグループのシステム障害についてまとめてみた
item.description: Source: piyolog / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach / Topics: Ransomware, Data Breach / Priority: High (73) / SOC: 61 / Confidence: High (88) / Focus: incident / breach (rank 3) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: incident / breach, JA attack activity, JA Japan/Asia relevance, target and impact context, uncertain reporting, Japan related, primary references 1 / Summary: 2026年7月13日、株式会社ニチレイは、同社グループのシステムが不正アクセスによる障害を受け、冷蔵倉庫の入出庫業務や冷凍食品の出荷業務に影響が生じていると公表しました。その後、同社のサーバーがサイバー攻撃を受けたことを確認したとして続報を公表し、障害の影響は取引先・関連組織の商品供給にも及んでいます。ここでは関連する情報をまとめます。 障害は冷蔵倉庫の入出庫業務にも影響 ニチレイは持株会社であり、事業会社として食品事業を担うニチレイフーズと、低温物流事業を担うニチレイロジグループなどを傘下に持つ(出典: 14)。株式会社ニチレイは2026年7月13日、「当社グループでのシステム障害発生について」を公表し、不正アクセスによるシステム障害が発生したことを明らかにした(出典: 1)。ニチレイの広報は取材に対し、社内のシステム部門からの報告でシステム障害を確認したのは同日午前6時50分ごろだったと説明している(出典: 6, 7)。 システム障害の影響は、ニチレイロジグループ各社が担う冷蔵倉庫の入出庫業務と、ニチレイフーズの冷凍食品出荷業務に及び、現時点では障害範囲は国内に限定されるとした(出典: 1)。ニチレイは発生同日に緊急対策本部を設置し、顧客・取引先の個人情報や顧客データの保護を最優先に、グループで使用するシステムの遮断措置を講じたことを、7月15日公表の第2報で明らかにした(出典 / Primary source: piyolog https://piyolog.hatenadiary.jp/entry/2026/07/16/022032 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: 2026年7月に起きたクレジットカード決済の全国的障害についてまとめてみた
item.description: Source: piyolog / Source attributes: Japan / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Mobile, Incident Response / Topics: Network Edge / Priority: High (73) / SOC: 52 / Confidence: High (88) / Focus: incident / breach (rank 3) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: incident / breach, JA detection/analysis, JA Japan/Asia relevance, JA critical sector, indicator context, uncertain reporting, Japan related, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: 2026年7月16日午前、全国各地でクレジットカード決済ができない、または利用しづらい障害が発生し、コンビニエンスストアや鉄道の券売機、ECサイト、モバイル決済など幅広い場面に影響が及びました。決済基盤を運営する日本カードネットワーク(CARDNET)や関係するカード会社、国際ブランドの担当者がそれぞれ状況について説明していますが、発信元によって説明の対象・内容は異なり、これらが同一の原因を指しているかは確認できていません。CARDNET自身は自社の障害ではないとしています。ここでは関連する情報をまとめます。 コンビニ・鉄道・ECサイトなど全国でカード決済不可に 2026年7月16日午前8時ごろから、全国各地でクレジットカード決済ができない、または利用しづらい事象が相次いで確認された。LIXILは「全国的なクレジットカード決済ネットワーク(CARDNET)の通信障害が発生しております」として8時ごろからECサイトの決済エラーが起きていると告知し(出典: 8)、サンドラッグも公式Xで「2026年7月16日午前8時ごろから、クレジットカードシステム障害が発生しております」と告知した(出典: 9) *1 。中部しんきんカードは8時頃の発生を、武蔵野ワークスは「今朝8時過ぎより」の発生をそれぞれ告知している(出典: 3, 15)。このほかWINTICKET(日本トーター)が8時10分頃から / Primary source: piyolog https://piyolog.hatenadiary.jp/entry/2026/07/17/065349 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: None and done? kittykatkrew’s short-lived ransomware play
item.description: Source: Barracuda Labs / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Ransomware, Data Breach / Topics: Ransomware, Malware, Data Breach / Priority: Medium (54) / SOC: 46 / Confidence: High (93) / Focus: incident / breach (rank 3) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: incident / breach, EN attack activity, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: Kittykatkrew emerged briefly in early 2026, pairing real BlackMatter‑family ransomware tooling with unverified victim claims and a short‑lived leak site. Despite credible malware samples, no listed victims have confirmed a breach, raising questions about whether the group’s activity was more about building reputation than executing successful attacks. The case highlights how new ransomware actors can generate attention—and real-world impact—even when their claims remain unproven. / Primary source: Barracuda Labs https://blog.barracuda.com/2026/07/16/none-and-done-kitty-kat-krew-ransomware-group / Primary source age: 0 days
